events.title: TechFest North Carolina (Triangle)
events.summary: NC TECH's annual flagship conference for North Carolina's technology sector, featuring industry speakers, company showcases, and networking across the full Triangle ecosystem. Confirmed May 13, 2026 on the nctech.org events calendar.

events.title: WomenHack Raleigh
events.summary: A diversity-focused recruiting event connecting women in tech with Triangle-area hiring managers for networking, resume reviews, and on-the-spot interviews. Confirmed May 13, 2026 on Eventbrite; open to job-seekers and employers alike.

events.title: Artsplosure — The Raleigh Arts Festival
events.summary: Raleigh's flagship free outdoor arts festival draws 70,000+ visitors to Fayetteville Street with 170+ juried artists, multiple live music stages, and food vendors. A mainstay of the downtown social calendar every third weekend of May since 1979.

events.title: American Dance Festival
events.summary: One of the world's most prestigious modern dance festivals, hosted at Duke University each June–July for a five-week run featuring internationally acclaimed companies and public workshops. A signature piece of Durham's arts identity since the festival moved here in 1978.

events.title: First Friday Raleigh
events.summary: A free monthly evening art walk through Raleigh's arts districts on the first Friday of every month, with gallery openings, food trucks, street performers, and pop-up shops. One of the easiest low-key ways for newcomers to explore downtown and meet locals.

events.title: Museum of Life and Science — Summer Season
events.location: Museum of Life and Science, Durham
events.summary: Durham's 84-acre outdoor science museum ramps up summer programming in early June, with the Butterfly House, Dinosaur Trail, live animal encounters, and adventure play areas ideal for all ages. Extended summer hours and weekly themed activities make it a repeat Triangle family destination.

events.title: Juneteenth Community Celebrations
events.summary: Both Raleigh and Durham host free outdoor events every June 19 marking the federal Juneteenth holiday, including cultural performances, art exhibitions, food, and family programming. A meaningful opportunity to engage with the Triangle's vibrant communities and rich history.

events.title: Eno River Festival (Festival for the Eno)
events.location: West Point on the Eno, Durham
events.summary: A beloved three-day July 4th weekend festival at West Point on the Eno park, featuring folk, blues, and bluegrass music, artisan crafts, river walks, and local food vendors. Running since 1980, it's widely considered the Triangle's most iconic community outdoor gathering.
